input.title: Variable Shadowing
input.error_message: No error. Prints 50 instead of global 100 if the intent was to use the global.

output.technical: The local variable `count_0` declared inside `print()` shadows the global variable of the same name. Changes or accesses within the function apply only to the local variable.
output.beginner: You created a new variable with the same name `count_0` inside the function, hiding the original one from outside.
output.analogy: If you have a friend named John in the room and someone yells 'Hey John!', your friend will answer, even if the president (also named John) is in the next room over.
output.solution: Remove the `int ` type declaration inside the function to use the global variable, or rename the local variable.
output.prevention: Avoid naming local variables identically to global variables or class members. Turn on `-Wshadow` compiler warnings.

output.technical: The local variable `height_38` declared inside `print()` shadows the global variable of the same name. Changes or accesses within the function apply only to the local variable.
output.beginner: You created a new variable with the same name `height_38` inside the function, hiding the original one from outside.
output.analogy: If you have a friend named John in the room and someone yells 'Hey John!', your friend will answer, even if the president (also named John) is in the next room over.
output.solution: Remove the `int ` type declaration inside the function to use the global variable, or rename the local variable.
output.prevention: Avoid naming local variables identically to global variables or class members. Turn on `-Wshadow` compiler warnings.
